PLAINTIFF MARK HAMMOND'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND PLAINTIFF'S SECOND SET OF REQUESTS FOR IDENTIFICATION AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED IN DEFENDANT'S FAVOR ON 9/10/10. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Not reported

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-10-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Cathy Mosbrucker of Mosbrucker & Foran (398.9880) for plaintiff and responding party; David Semel of Haapala Thompson & Abern, LLP (510.763.2324) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER SUAREZ'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof and plaintiff raises no triable issue of material fact. The litigation privilege is not applicable, however the release bars this action. Paragraph 8 of the release states that plaintiff releases all claims except those stated in paragraph 10. Paragraph 10 only applies where defendant's family moves from the premises within 36 months of April 3, 2007. Plaintiff and his counsel agreed to that narrow exception and it does not apply to the instant case. The claims of duress, undue influence, and unconscionability fail. Plaintiff had counsel and received $20,000 in exchange for the release. The exception at paragraph 10 is not ambiguous and parole evidence is disallowed. The exception is not reasonably susceptible to plaintiff's proffered meaning that he retained all rights under the SFAC and was allowed to sue if defendant's family did not move into the premises within a certain time. Plaintiff's claim of fraud fails because a party cannot rely on the fraud exception to the parole evidence rule where the alleged misrepresentation contradicts an express term. See Banco Do Brazil, S.A. v. Lation, Inc. (1991) 234 Cal. App. 3d 973, 1009. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
